---
title: "RP 12: Smart, Not Strong"
format: html
---
# RP 12: Smart, Not Strong

Recovery is not a test of toughness. The "smart, not strong" principle means you reduce risk through planning, structure, and support rather than trying to overpower every urge with willpower alone. Willpower is limited when stress, fatigue, and emotion are high; strategy remains reliable.

People who do well long-term tend to simplify decisions: fewer high-risk exposures, clearer routines, faster response to warning signs, and consistent accountability.

A core principle of staying alcohol-free is to shape your environment and routines so healthy choices are easier than return-to-use patterns.

## Strategy Upgrade Worksheet
| Situation where I rely on willpower | Smarter system change I can make | Start date |
|---|---|---|
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |
|  |  |  |

::: {.callout-important}
### Practice plan

Work through this in real-life situations rather than only reading it.

Implement one system change this week and track whether urges become easier to manage.

If something feels hard, simplify the step and repeat it consistently.
:::
